Document:
# Lawyer
1. Lawyers report directly to the Head of Personnel.
2. Lawyers must report to court when summoned for a trial, and are expected to provide legal counsel to their clients, and other duties as assigned.
3. When arbitrating petty disputes between Central Command-employed personnel, Lawyers are expected to be impartial and provide fair mediation to all parties involved.
4. Unless requested by a member of Security or Command, Lawyers are not expected to involve themselves in the legal matters of prisoners outside of plea bargains, and must defer to Internal Affairs Agents in these matters.
5. The Head of Personnel retains the right to dismiss a Lawyer from their position immediately, without need for justification or a dismissal form.
